name: PotlatchDeltic
cat: Forest Products & Real Estate / Timber REIT

political.s: PotlatchDeltic has documented right-leaning political leanings with $90K in FEC donations showing 86% Republican support (rep%=34000/39500). The company operates as a timber REIT in politically sensitive industries related to forest management and land use.

charity.s: PotlatchDeltic demonstrates active charitable engagement focused on communities where it operates. The company reported nearly $600,000 in charitable contributions in 2024, supporting education, community programs, conservation, and local initiatives through direct grants and employee volunteer efforts.
charity.focus: community programs, education, conservation, local fire departments, youth sports

environment.s: PotlatchDeltic maintains third-party certifications (SFI and FSC standards) for 100% of forestry practices and wood products procurement. The company reports substantial environmental practices including planting 29 million seedlings, protecting 7,880 miles of rivers and streams (2024), and managing 2.1 million acres under sustainable forestry protocols with robust environmental compliance systems.

labor.s: The company emphasizes safety with incident and severity rates below industry averages and has implemented training and apprenticeship programs. However, a 2018 lawsuit alleged unpaid overtime wages and improper time-rounding practices at its Warren, Arkansas mill, suggesting potential wage compliance issues despite stated safety commitments.

dei.s: PotlatchDeltic reports expanded Board diversity and employee resource groups but maintains a neutral-to-mixed stance. The company emphasizes inclusive culture and diverse hiring in ESG reports while operating in traditionally male-dominated forestry and timber sectors with limited explicit DEI leadership.

animals.s: Not applicable for forest products and timber REIT company.

guns.s: Not applicable. PotlatchDeltic is a forest products and real estate company with no involvement in firearms manufacturing, sales, or distribution.

privacy.s: Limited publicly available information on company data privacy practices. Company reports on environmental, social, and governance matters but does not prominently disclose data privacy policies or customer/employee privacy frameworks.

execPay.s: Executive compensation data shows CEO severance arrangements (e.g., $6.7 million in 2026 merger severance) and equity grants exceeding $3 million annually. Glassdoor employees rate compensation 4.3/5 stars, suggesting mixed satisfaction relative to executive packages. Specific CEO-to-worker pay ratio not disclosed.

fec.committeeName: POTLATCHDELTIC CORPORATION POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (POTLATCHDELTIC PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
